description: Additional reproductive, toxicology, cardiac, and oncology coverage for the dog triage benchmark.

cases.description: Nest-making with mammary enlargement in an intact female should open reproductive questioning.

messages.content: My unspayed dog is carrying toys around, nesting, and her mammary area looks enlarged.

cases.description: Prolonged heat-style spotting should open reproductive questioning.

messages.content: My dog has had spotting from her vulva for a lot longer than usual.

cases.description: Postpartum mammary pain and redness should open reproductive follow-up.

messages.content: She had puppies recently and one of her mammary glands is red, hard, and painful.

cases.description: Scrotal swelling without collapse should open reproductive questioning.

messages.content: My dog has swelling around his testicles and keeps licking there.

cases.description: Stable vaginal discharge should open reproductive questioning when no collapse signs are present.

messages.content: There is some discharge from my dog's vulva but she is still eating and acting okay.

cases.description: Green discharge before a puppy is delivered should escalate.

messages.content: My dog in labor has green discharge and no puppy has come out yet.

cases.description: Heavy postpartum bleeding should escalate.

messages.content: She had puppies yesterday and now she is bleeding heavily and seems weak.

cases.description: Suspected retained puppy with shock signs should escalate.

messages.content: She finished having puppies but now seems weak, distressed, and still has strong contractions.

cases.description: Foul discharge with systemic illness in an intact female should escalate.

messages.content: My unspayed dog has smelly discharge, is drinking a lot, and feels feverish and miserable.

cases.description: Penis stuck out with swelling and distress should escalate.

messages.content: My dog's penis is stuck out, swollen, and he is very uncomfortable.

cases.description: Small chocolate exposure without symptoms should still open triage questioning.

messages.content: My little dog may have eaten some milk chocolate but seems normal right now.

cases.description: Possible marijuana exposure with mild signs should open toxin triage.

messages.content: My dog got into an edible and now seems sleepy and wobbly.

cases.description: Garlic or onion ingestion should open toxin triage when no collapse signs are present.

messages.content: My dog ate a bunch of oniony leftovers and now I am worried.

cases.description: Possible ibuprofen ingestion should open toxin questioning immediately.

messages.content: I think my dog may have eaten one of my ibuprofen pills.

cases.description: Possible antifreeze ingestion should escalate immediately.

messages.content: My dog licked green fluid from the garage floor and is now staggering and drooling.

cases.description: Sago palm ingestion should escalate immediately.

messages.content: My dog chewed on a sago palm and is vomiting now.

cases.description: Grape ingestion with vomiting or weakness should escalate.

messages.content: He ate a bunch of grapes and now is vomiting and acting weak.

cases.description: Household toxin exposure with GI signs and weakness should escalate.

messages.content: My dog got into a toxic plant and now is vomiting and seems really weak.

cases.description: Tremors after eating moldy food should escalate immediately.

messages.content: He got into moldy trash and now he is shaking hard and cannot walk right.

cases.description: Night cough with stable behavior should open cardiac-respiratory questioning.

messages.content: My older dog coughs more at night and after resting.

cases.description: Exercise intolerance without collapse should open cardiac questioning.

messages.content: My dog tires out much faster on walks and wants to stop early.

cases.description: Gradual abdominal fluid suspicion should open questioning when no crisis signs are present.

messages.content: His belly seems to be filling out and he gets tired more easily lately.

cases.description: Single recovered fainting spell should open urgent cardiac questioning.

messages.content: My dog briefly fainted on a walk but came back around quickly.

cases.description: Collapse with blue or gray gums should escalate immediately.

messages.content: My dog collapsed and his gums looked blue-gray.

cases.description: Resting respiratory distress in a heart-disease style presentation should escalate.

messages.content: He has known heart disease and now is breathing hard even while lying still.

cases.description: Collapse after excitement with delayed recovery should escalate.

messages.content: My dog got excited, collapsed, and still seems weak and out of it.

cases.description: Rapid breathing with pallor and weakness should escalate.

messages.content: She is breathing fast, looks pale, and can barely stand up.

cases.description: Stable new skin lump should open mass characterization questioning.

messages.content: I found a round new lump on my dog's side and it does not seem painful.

cases.description: Weight loss plus a new mass should open serious systemic questioning.

messages.content: My dog has been losing weight and I also found a lump near his ribs.

cases.description: Possible oral mass with otherwise stable behavior should open questioning.

messages.content: There seems to be a growth in my dog's mouth and his breath is terrible.

cases.description: Gradual limb pain and swelling should open orthopedic-oncology questioning.

messages.content: My big dog has a swollen painful leg and a limp that has been getting worse for weeks.

cases.description: Multiple enlarged lymph-node style swellings should open systemic questioning.

messages.content: I can feel several swollen lumps under my dog's jaw and behind his knees.

cases.description: Acute collapse with abdominal distension in a cancer-risk presentation should escalate.

messages.content: My older dog suddenly collapsed, has a swollen belly, and his gums are very pale.

cases.description: Heavy bleeding from an oral mass should escalate.

messages.content: There is a growth in my dog's mouth and now it is bleeding a lot.

cases.description: Neck swelling causing breathing or swallowing trouble should escalate.

messages.content: My dog has a large throat lump and now he is struggling to swallow and breathe.

cases.description: Severe nosebleed with weakness should escalate.

messages.content: My dog has a major nosebleed and is now weak and shaky.
